Tafseer:
1. Research of Words and Explanation of Verses
(Whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth exalts Allah, and He is the Exalted in Might, the Wise) (1).
(Connection of the Verse of Tasbeeh with the Subsequent Subject). This verse can function both in a context of encouragement and motivation, as well as an expression of Allah's absolute self-sufficiency. Further ahead, the hypocrites who shirk Jihad are reproached for their breach of promise and cowardice. This verse connects with the subsequent text from both of these mentioned aspects. If viewed from the perspective of motivation and encouragement, the meaning would be: when everything in this universe is actively engaged in the glorification and servitude of God—Who is dominant over all things and Wise in every action—how tragic it is if anyone shrinks from striving in His path! If viewed from the perspective of self-sufficiency, the meaning would be: when everything in this universe is engaged in glorifying and serving Allah, and He alone is the truly Dominant and Wise, why would He care if a few cowards slink away from striving in His path? It is also worth noting that in this Surah, the past tense 'Sabbaha' (exalted) is used, while the next Surah uses the present/future tense 'Yusabbihu' (exalts). The difference between these two styles is that the past tense denotes an established fact and reality, whereas the present/future tense denotes the current state and continuity.

The mention of the two attributes (Al-Aziz Al-Hakim) without a conjunction indicates that both attributes coexist simultaneously in the One being described. That is, He is simultaneously dominant and all-powerful over everything, and there is wisdom and purpose in every act of His. The meaning is that no entity is more worthy of trust than the Being characterized by these attributes! Unfortunate are those who do not place their trust even in such a Being.

(O you who have believed, why do you say what you do not do? Most hateful it is in the sight of Allah that you say what you do not do) (2, 3).
(Warning to the Warriors of the Tongue). Although the address is general in its wording, it is specifically directed at those who made loud claims of faith, Islam, spending in the way of God, and Jihad, but proved to be completely cowardly when the time of trial arrived. Addressing them, He said: Why do you claim that which you do not have the courage to fulfill? If your delusion is that you are earning some virtue through this verbal Jihad, then this thought is completely wrong. This attitude of yours does not bring you virtue near Allah, but rather causes further wrath, because you are a great warrior by tongue but a total zero in terms of action. The entire value and worth of a man's word lies in his loyalty and truthfulness. If a person flatly refuses a seeker of help at the very first stage by saying he will not help him, then although this refusal—if he has the capacity to help—is contrary to chivalry, empathy, and Islamic brotherhood, and is a disliked act near Allah, yet far more detestable to Allah is the one who generously reassured a needy person of help, but deceived him when the time came to fulfill the promise.

While this attitude is abhorrent and detestable in every form, its severity multiplies when it involves a Prophet and those who claim to believe in him. Those who do not accept the call of a Prophet deserve Allah's wrath, and rightly so, but even more deserving of Allah's wrath are those who accept everything he says by tongue but do not act upon anything, or act only on things that align with their personal desires and hide away when a crucial stage arises. Clearly, the attitude of these people is more dangerous to religion than that of open enemies, because they manifested enmity toward religion by pretending to be friends, and committed treason after enlisting in its army.

Another reason for them being more subject to divine wrath is that once they accepted the religion, they effectively acknowledged that this matter appeals to their hearts and is understandable to them. From this perspective, their case becomes entirely different from those who oppose it on the grounds that they have not understood the matter at all. Not understanding a matter is, after all, an excuse due to which a person may be deemed worthy of leeway, but those who have already confessed to understanding it—if they evade its practical demands—the cause can only be their moral weakness, which can never serve as an excuse for anyone. Rather, it is a vice in every respect that will only bring about Allah's wrath and His detestation.

(The Real Weakness of the Hypocrites). (Indeed, Allah loves those who fight in His cause in a row as though they are a structure joined firmly) (4). This uncovers the specific weakness of those hypocrites who were censured. It becomes clear that their distinct weakness was that to impose an impression of their loyalty and self-sacrifice, they would express great enthusiasm for Jihad, but when Jihad was commanded, they would hide away. In Surah An-Nisa, their weakness is pointed out in these words: (Have you not seen those who were told, 'Restrain your hands and establish prayer and give zakah'? But then when fighting was ordained for them, at once a party of them feared men as they fear Allah or with even greater fear.) (An-Nisa: 4, 77).

In Surah At-Tawbah, these same people are reproached in these words:
(O you who have believed, what is the matter with you that, when you are told to go forth in the cause of Allah, you adhere heavily to the earth?) (At-Tawbah 9:38).

The state of these people's cowardice was such that even if they were forced to go out for war, they lacked the courage to fight lined up in rows and shielding their chests; rather, their continuous effort was to somehow keep themselves safe from the danger of war. In Surah At-Tawbah, the picture of their cowardice is drawn like this:
(If they could find a refuge or caves or a place to enter, they would turn to it while they run wildly.) (At-Tawbah 9:57).

Keeping these weaknesses of theirs in view, it was said that the method you have adopted to please Allah merely through verbal lip-service, instead of pleasing Him, will cause a further increase in His wrath and anger. Allah will never love those who boast a lot but possess no courage for sacrifice within themselves. He loves only those who stand in the battlefield like a lead-reinforced wall and face the enemy eye-to-eye.

(Bunyan) means a building, but here it refers to a wall. Even if a single brick of a wall shifts from its place, it becomes easy to tear down the entire wall. For this reason, a warning was given in Surah Al-Anfal that if a person leaves his place from the row and flees during an organized military campaign, he returns with the wrath of Allah and his refuge is Hell: (he has returned with anger from Allah, and his refuge is Hell - and wretched is the destination.) (Al-Anfal 8:16).

(And when Moses said to his people, 'O my people, why do you harm me while you certainly know that I am the messenger of Allah to you?' And when they deviated, Allah caused their hearts to deviate. And Allah does not guide the defiantly disobedient people) (5).
(The Example of the Hypocrites' Conduct). The end result of this conduct of the hypocrites is explained through an example showing that this is the exact conduct of the Jews. They would express great passion and fervor for Jihad, but when the time of trial arrived, they would drop out. When Prophet Moses (peace be upon him) commanded them to attack the Palestinians, they sat back giving this answer: 'These are very powerful people, we are not ready to become the food for their swords. You and your Lord go and fight them; we will not dare enter it as long as they do not vacate this city.'

Their conduct remained the same regarding the war mentioned in Surah Al-Baqarah, where they had requested Prophet Samuel (peace be upon him) with great enthusiasm to choose a leader for Jihad, but when the leader was selected, they refused to fight under his leadership. If you study the Torah, you will find that not a single occasion passed where they obeyed Prophet Moses (peace be upon him) with complete willingness. Prophet Moses (peace be upon him) repeatedly expressed his grief and anger at this conduct of theirs with great heartache, and reproached them in very harsh words, but no change occurred in their conduct until the very end; rather, the hardness of their hearts kept increasing.

(O my people, why do you harm me while you certainly know that I am the messenger of Allah to you). This refers to those complaints of Prophet Moses (peace be upon him) which he made repeatedly over the breaches of covenant and acts of disobedience by his people, and with which the Torah is filled. The Jews had no doubt about the prophethood of Prophet Moses (peace be upon him). They knew very well that he was the Messenger of Allah, but despite this, purely due to their low morale, world-seeking, and baseness, knowing that he was Allah's Messenger, they continuously disobeyed him.

(And when they deviated, Allah caused their hearts to deviate) This is the punishment that Allah the Almighty gave them in accordance with His Sunnah (divine law): that even after repeated warnings and reminders, they did not straighten the direction of their hearts, so Allah turned their hearts crooked in the very direction they preferred for themselves. It is a characteristic of human nature that a man's nature turns toward whichever direction he steers it. If you turn it toward a wrong direction contrary to reason and innate nature, it will initially resist turning toward it, but if you persist in turning it, it will eventually be molded into that shape.

(The Divine Law Regarding Guidance and Misguidance). Allah Almighty's dealing with man in the matter of guidance and misguidance is according to this very principle of his nature. Allah Almighty forces neither guidance nor misguidance upon anyone. He granted freedom to man by giving him the awareness of good and evil, so that he may choose either the path of good or evil. If he chooses the path of good, He grants him the grace of success, and if he wishes to go on the path of evil, He gives him respite in it, if He wills. (And Allah does not guide the defiantly disobedient people) meaning it is not the rule of Allah the Almighty to forcefully thrust guidance upon someone who prefers misguidance. Guidance is granted only to the one who values it and becomes a seeker of it.

As we have indicated, this is a warning to the hypocrites that if you follow this same conduct of the Jews, your hearts will also be made crooked just as their hearts were made crooked. Then you will always walk on the wrong path and will be deprived of the ability to walk on the straight path forever.

(And when Jesus, the son of Mary, said, 'O children of Israel, indeed I am the messenger of Allah to you confirming what came before me of the Torah and bringing good tidings of a messenger to come after me, whose name is Ahmad.' But when he came to them with clear evidences, they said, 'This is obvious magic.') (6).
(Effects of the Crookedness of Heart which Manifested Later). Now it is being explained that it is the effect of the crookedness of their hearts that they were deprived of believing in Prophet Jesus (peace be upon him), and they are busy day and night in triviality to deny the final Messenger whose glad tidings he (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him) gave, even though despite all their opposition, this light of Allah will manifest in its perfection; neither they nor the polytheists of Quraish can harm it at all.

Prophet Jesus (peace be upon him) is the last prophet of the Children of Israel. The purpose of mentioning him is to show that the treatment meted out to Prophet Moses (peace be upon him) by the Children of Israel, and the curse that befell them as its retribution, its evil effect dominated them until the end, to the extent that as a result, they even denied the Messiah (peace be upon him), and the opposition they are showing to Islam now is also an effect of that. The point is that this disease is so fatal that once it catches on, it is impossible to get rid of it. Safety lies only in not letting its contagion touch you.

(Confirming what came before me of the Torah) Prophet Jesus (peace be upon him) stated this as a proof of his missing-ship: that my appearance has confirmed those prophecies which are present in the scriptures of the Torah before me. The clearest glad tiding about Prophet Jesus (peace be upon him) was given by his predecessor Prophet John (peace be upon him). He defined his specific mission as coming to give glad tidings of the one coming after him. In the context of this glad tiding, he also referred to those prophecies which already existed regarding Prophet Jesus (peace be upon him) and because of which people were waiting for the advent of a prophet. All these things have been explained in their respective places; there is no room for repetition here.

(The Correct Meaning of 'Mussadiqan Lima'). The literal meaning of (Mussadiqan lima bayna yadayya) has been discussed in detail in its place. Those who take its meaning as 'I am one who certifies the Torah' have neither understood the context of this discourse nor have they been able to determine the correct literal meaning of 'Mussadiq'. The Messiah (peace be upon him) did not say this to show himself as a believer in the Torah, but rather, as we have indicated, as a proof of his prophethood and messenger-ship, and wherever this word appears in the Quran, it appears in this context. For this purpose, it is completely futile to say 'I am one who certifies the Torah'. If he certified the Torah, it would only prove that he believed in the Torah, but how would his prophethood and messenger-ship be proven by that? Furthermore, bear in mind that neither the Messiah (peace be upon him) nor Muhammad, the Messenger of Allah (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him), certified every part of the Torah; rather, the Messiah (peace be upon him) exposed its alterations, and the Quran did too—in fact, the Quran thoroughly exposed them.

(The Importance of the Messiah's Prophecies Regarding the Prophet). (And bringing good tidings of a messenger to come after me, whose name is Ahmad). That is, along with it, the Messiah (peace be upon him) stated his mission: 'I have come to give the glad tidings of a messenger who will come after me and his name will be Ahmad.' As far as the prophecy of this messenger is concerned, it dates back to the time of our master Abraham (peace be upon him), the mention of which is also coming in the next Surah. After Prophet Abraham (peace be upon him), Prophet Moses (peace be upon him) also gave the glad tidings of this messenger. Prophecies are also present in various scriptures of the Torah from some other prophets, and whether the People of the Book accept their application to the Prophet (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him) or not, an unbiased mind is compelled to accept that they cannot apply to anyone else except the Prophet (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him). In short, as far as the glad tiding of the Prophet (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him) is concerned, Prophet Jesus (peace be upon him) is not unique in it, but his glad tiding is entirely different from other previous glad tidings from three perspectives. First, he did not make this glad tiding a secondary task but rather the specific purpose and mission of his advent. Second, he also determined the time of its appearance absolutely decisively. Third, he gave this glad tiding with the explicit mention of the Prophet's respected name, Ahmad. These three aspects are quite important, which is why we will discuss them briefly here.

(The Wisdom Behind Naming the Gospel as 'Injeel'). The first point, i.e., Prophet Jesus (peace be upon him) being commissioned with the glad tiding of the Prophet (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him), is clear from his words (bringing good tidings of a messenger to come after me), but a greater proof is the name of the scripture itself that was revealed to him. The word 'Injeel' is unanimously Greek, meaning 'glad tidings' by consensus. The question is, why out of all heavenly scriptures was this specific scripture named with this word? Christians, based on their self-fabricated belief regarding salvation, might justify it by saying that it is a glad tiding of salvation for mankind, but this justification is completely childish. There may be some convoluted argument for the belief concocted by Paul in his absurd theology, but there is not even the weakest argument for it in these Gospels. Just as the mission of the prophets (peace be upon them) has been to give glad tidings, it has also been to warn—even to a greater degree—and both duties were performed by Prophet Jesus (peace be upon him) just as they were performed by every other prophet. He gave glad tidings of success and salvation to those who believed, and warned those who disbelieved of Hellfire; then there was no reason why his scripture should be specifically named 'Glad Tiding' (Injeel). However, the reason for it being named so could be that the distinctive trait and special mission of Prophet Jesus (peace be upon him) was indeed to give glad tidings of the final Messenger coming after him. He was sent commissioned by Allah Almighty for this mission. For this reason, the name of his scripture became Glad Tiding (Injeel) so that his mission is expressed through the name itself, and this name will become an argument against the Christians on the Day of Judgment: that they remained deprived of believing in the one whose glad tidings he was commissioned by Allah Almighty to give, and the parable of the Messiah (peace be upon him) regarding the virgins applied to them—that they kept waiting for the bridegroom all night lighting their lamps, but when the time came for the bridegroom to arrive, the oil in their flasks finished and they fell asleep.

If you study the Gospels with deep reflection, you will find that their special subject is the glad tiding of the Kingdom of Heaven. This subject appears repeatedly in them through different styles, expressions, and diverse parables. If one reflects well on the characteristics of this heavenly kingdom, the concept given of its gradual development, and the traits mentioned of those who enter it and those who are deprived of it, it becomes clearly visible that this is in reality the glad tiding of the prophethood that manifested in the form of the advent of the Prophet (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him) and the good news of that righteous system whose blessings spread across the earth through the hands of the Companions (may Allah be pleased with them). There is no room to explain this issue here, otherwise, I would have clarified my viewpoint well by giving examples. However, I hope that those who ponder over the Gospels from this angle will agree with my opinion that their real subject is indeed the glad tiding of the heavenly kingdom that was established through the blessed hands of the Prophet (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him).

(This Prophecy is with the Specification of Time). The specification of time in this prophecy also greatly increases its importance. Before this, however many prophecies occurred regarding the Prophet (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him) or other prophets (peace be upon them), none of them specified the time with this explicitness; rather, all related to an indefinite future. But in this prophecy, it is explicitly stated that the messenger will come after him, and he has come to give his glad tidings and clear the way for the one to come. Now the question is, who is that personality who appeared in this entire world after our master Messiah (peace be upon him) to whom those attributes or parables could apply which he stated regarding him or the system to be established through his hands? Obstinacy and stubbornness are different matters, but the fact is that no name other than Muhammad (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him) can be taken in answer to this. If Christians do not want to take this name, then they must also renounce the Gospels. After all, how long will they continue to give a glad tiding whose fulfillment has not appeared in two thousand years?

(Glad Tiding with Explicitness of Name). The third aspect of the issue is also highly important, rather the most important: that Prophet Jesus (peace be upon him) gave the glad tiding of the Prophet (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him) with the explicit mention of his name. The appearance of this prophecy with the clarity of the name is proof that Allah Almighty willed that no ambiguity should remain in it, but rather everything should come forward so openly that the stubbornness of those who deny it is completely exposed and Allah's argument is firmly established against them.

(The Audacity of the People of the Book in Altering Names). But the havoc wrought by the alterers upon names is most painful. They either distorted the shape of these names in such a way that no possibility remained of knowing their original form, or they translated them, and then translated the translations, removing them so far from reality that digging out their research became equivalent to bringing forth a river of milk (an impossible task).

As an example of this, we present 'Murdah' and 'Bakkah'. Both of these names have appeared in the Torah. 'Murdah' is that place of sacrifice where Prophet Abraham (peace be upon him) offered the sacrifice of Prophet Ishmael (peace be upon him), and 'Bakkah' (Makkah) is that place where Prophet Abraham (peace be upon him) settled Prophet Ishmael (peace be upon him) and from whose progeny he prayed for the advent of a messenger. Since the Jews were not prepared to tolerate under any circumstances that any connection of Prophet Abraham (peace be upon him) be proven with Makkah, or that the son to be sacrificed be deemed Prophet Ishmael (peace be upon him), for this reason, they brutally scraped and erased every sign from the Torah which they found contrary to their desire. Consequently, their scholars and reciters turned 'Murdah' into Morah, Moriah, Moriah, and who knows what else through the twisting of the tongue. Similarly, they turned 'Bakkah' into 'Baka', and then giving it the name of the Valley of Baka, translators translated it as the Valley of Weeping, even though Bakkah and Makkah are the same thing. The research on this has passed in its place in this book. 1 (See Tadabbur-e-Quran, Volume I, pages 747, 748).

From these two examples, one can estimate what befell those names in the Torah and Gospel which the Jews or Christians found contrary to their purpose. Especially 'Ahmad' or 'Hamd' are such names that they could not be prepared to tolerate even a single letter of them. Thus, it is clearly known from the Gospels that the Messiah (peace be upon him) gave the glad tiding of the Prophet's advent with the explicit mention of his name, but along with it, it also becomes clear that the Christian scholars showed such mastery in putting a veil over this name that no one except the Jews can rival them in this mastery.

First, we quote those references from the Gospel of John in which the glad tiding of the Prophet (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him) is given, after which we will draw attention to their specific aspects. Our master Messiah (peace be upon him) says: 'And I will pray the Father, and he shall give you another Comforter, that he may abide with you for ever; Even the Spirit of truth' (John 14:16-17).
'But the Comforter, which is the Holy Ghost, whom the Father will send in my name, he shall teach you all things, and bring all things to your remembrance, whatsoever I have said unto you' (John 14:26).
'Hereafter I will not talk much with you: for the prince of this world cometh, and hath nothing in me' (John 14:30).
'But when the Comforter is come, whom I will send unto you from the Father, even the Spirit of truth, which proceedeth from the Father, he shall testify of me' (John 15:26).
'Nevertheless I tell you the truth; It is expedient for you that I go away: for if I go not away, the Comforter will not come unto you' (John 16:7).
'I have yet many things to say unto you, but ye cannot bear them now. Howbeit when he, the Spirit of truth, is come, he will guide you into all truth: for he shall not speak of himself; but whatsoever he shall hear, that shall he speak: and he will shew you things to come' (John 16:12-13).

These references we have taken from the Gospel of John, which is accepted among all sects of Christians. In the Gospel of Barnabas, the glad tiding of the Prophet (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him) appears repeatedly with the explicit mention of your honored name, Muhammad, but we are not giving its references because the current Christians, who are followers of Paul, do not consider it authentic. Reflecting on the aforementioned references, a few things come forward very clearly. First, the Messiah (peace be upon him) gave the glad tiding of the Prophet (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him) with such clear attributes that they cannot apply to anyone else except the Prophet (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him), for example: 'he shall give you another Comforter, that he may abide with you for ever', 'he shall teach you all things', 'the prince of this world cometh, and hath nothing in me'. Reflect: who else is there after the Messiah (peace be upon him) except the Prophet (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him) who can be deemed the fulfillment of these attributes?

Second, although attempts were made to insert disconnected sentences and words before and after these prophecies in which their meaning could be muddled, the alterers did not achieve success in this attempt; rather, the disconnectedness of those sentences is completely prominent.

Third, it is clearly perceived that the Messiah (peace be upon him) stated a specific word in praise of the Prophet (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him) which subsequent translators and commentators made the specific target of their alteration and exerted their full strength to distance it as much as possible from its correct meaning. Someone translated it as Helper, someone as Advocate, someone as Intercessor, someone as the Spirit of Truth. As for what that word originally was, the Greek word used in John is said to be 'Paracletus', in explaining the meaning of which those subtle distortions were made that are mentioned above. This Greek word, clearly, must be a translation of some Syriac word because the original language of the Gospel was Syriac, so now who will research what it was? When an entire nation has been engaged for centuries in a struggle to make a word disappear, in whose capacity is it to track it down! It is the favor of the Quran that it gave us some clue to it. The research of some Muslim historians is that the original Syriac word is 'Munhamanna', which has the same meaning in Syriac as Muhammad and Ahmad.

(The Wickedness of the Jews). (But when he came to them with clear evidences, they said, 'This is obvious magic.') The subject of 'Jaa' (came) is Prophet Jesus (peace be upon him). That is, when he came with highly manifest miracles as the fulfillment of previous prophecies and as the bearer of glad tidings of the final Messenger to come after him, the hearts of the Jews had become so crooked that they declared all his miracles to be magic and denied him. It is explicitly stated in the Gospel that the Jews declared his miracles to be the feat of magic and also said that the support of the Holy Spirit has no part in what he shows, but rather he has the help of a demon, Beelzebub. Some people have deemed the subject of 'Jaa' to be the Prophet (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him), but this is completely against the context.

(A Delicate and Subtle Aspect). Here, also observe the difference that is prominent in the style of address of Prophet Moses (peace be upon him) and Prophet Jesus (peace be upon him). Prophet Moses (peace be upon him) addressed the Children of Israel as 'Ya Qawmi' (O my people), but Prophet Jesus (peace be upon him) addressed them as 'Ya Bani Isra'eel' (O children of Israel). The reason for this is obvious: he had no national lineage connection with the Children of Israel nor with any other nation; rather, he was Jesus, son of Mary. The Quran has observed even these delicate aspects of address.

(And who is more unjust than one who invents a lie about Allah while he is being invited to Islam? And Allah does not guide the wrongdoing people) (7). This is an expression of sorrow over the misfortune of the Jews that their own crookedness has now incited them to oppose Islam. It is said: Who can be more unjust to his own soul than those who are being invited to Islam, yet they fabricate various lies against Allah Almighty to invent excuses for opposing Islam?

(The Fabricated Claims of the Jews). (Invents a lie about Allah). This refers to those things which they had fabricated in their own praise to prove their greatness and superiority. For example, that 'we are a chosen nation and the focus of God's attention, how can we be in need of guidance from a prophet born among the unlettered? The household of Israel has always been specified for prophethood and messenger-ship, how can a prophet be born outside of it?' In this context, they had also fabricated the claim: 'We have this instruction that we should not confirm the claim to prophethood of any person unless fire descends from heaven to consume the sacrifice presented by him.' The Quran refuted all these fabricated claims in Surah Al-Baqarah, stating that all of this is fabrication; Allah has never said any such thing anywhere. Pointing toward them, it is said here how deprived of fortune are those people who are being invited to Islam while they themselves are throwing the dust of their own fabricated myths into their own eyes.

(And Allah does not guide the wrongdoing people) This points again toward that divine law which was explained in verse 5. It is said that Allah Almighty does not guide those people who inflict injustice upon their own souls. The meaning is that Allah did them the favor of giving them guidance beforehand regarding the coming prophet so that they might recognize him themselves and make others recognize him, but when they valued this favor by erasing the signs told by Allah themselves, then Allah does not grant guidance to such wretched ones.

(The Parable of the Jews' Futile Effort). (They want to extinguish the light of Allah with their mouths, but Allah will perfect His light, although the disbelievers dislike it) (8). This is a parable of that futile effort: attempting to suppress the guidance sent down by Allah with one's fabricated statements and self-invented innovations is just like attempting to blow out the sun or the moon with the breath of one's mouth. It is said that this effort of theirs will remain futile. Allah is perfecting His light. This will very soon turn from a crescent into a full moon, no matter how heavy this matter feels upon the disbelievers.

(It is He who as sent His Messenger with guidance and the religion of truth to manifest it over all religion, although those who associate others with Allah dislike it) (9).
(Explicit Declaration of the Dominance of Islam). This is an explanation of (but Allah will perfect His light, although the disbelievers dislike it) that the same God Who has decided to perfect His light has sent His Messenger with His guidance and His religion of truth so that He may make this religion dominant over all religions of this land, and this matter will bound to happen, no matter how disagreeable it is to the polytheists and regardless of how much power they exert against it.

In the verse above, it said (although the disbelievers dislike it) which is general in summary, including everyone who denied this religion of truth and this true Messenger. In this verse, the words are (although those who associate others with Allah dislike it) which are specifically for the polytheists of Quraish. Both of these words combined have encompassed all those opposing forces which were opposing Islam in Arabia at that time. It is as if all of them have been challenged: exert as much power as you want, but you will not be able to cause any harm to Allah's religion. The absolute decree of Allah is only that through this Messenger, His religion of truth will dominate over all religions of this land. Consequently, this matter was fulfilled. Very soon that time arrived when it was declared regarding the whole of Arabia that two religions will not coexist in this land; rather, only the religion of Allah will rule. This same subject has passed in Surah At-Tawbah in these words:
(They want to extinguish the light of Allah with their mouths, but Allah refuses except to perfect His light, although the disbelievers dislike it. It is He who has sent His Messenger with guidance and the religion of truth to manifest it over all religion, although those who associate others with Allah dislike it.) (At-Tawbah: 9, 32-33).

2. Subject of the Subsequent Verses 10-14
In the paragraph above, it was explained that it is completely wrong for the believers to make a covenant of hearing and obeying with the Messenger and then attempt to flee from it by getting trapped in the love of life and wealth when the time comes to fulfill the responsibilities of that covenant. Now it is being explained what the correct conduct is for them, by adopting which they can succeed in both this world and the Hereafter. Recite the verses... (Verses 10-14)

3. Research of Words and Explanation of Verses
(O you who have believed, shall I guide you to a transaction that will save you from a painful punishment?) (10). (Successful Transaction for the Believers). That is, after believing and making a covenant of hearing and obeying with the Messenger, if you want to know what the correct conduct is for you, come, let me tell you that bargain which will save you from a painful punishment. The thing which is expressed here as 'Tijarah' (bargain/transaction) is explained ahead: that you remain ready to sacrifice your life and your wealth in the way of Allah, and in return for it, become deserving of Allah's forgiveness and His Paradise. This is the greatest success, to achieve which you must risk your stakes. In this verse, its reward is stated only as saving you from the painful punishment of Hellfire, but in the subsequent verses, the clarification is coming that in its reward, you will also achieve all the triumphs of the Hereafter, and in this world too, you will achieve that great victory which you desire. Here, the mention of salvation from punishment alone is made because the real objective of a believer should be to remain safe from Allah's punishment; if this thing is achieved, it is as if everything is achieved.

(You believe in Allah and His Messenger and strive in the cause of Allah with your wealth and your lives. That is best for you, if you should know. He will forgive for you your sins and admit you into gardens beneath which rivers flow and pleasant dwellings in gardens of perpetual residence. That is the great success) (11, 12).
(Explanation of a Style of Arabic Grammar). We have been pointing out here and there to this style of Arabic grammar that when the subject of advice and sincere exhortation is to be produced within a command or prohibition, they are put in the narrative style. Consequently, in this verse, (Tu'minuna) [you believe] and (Tujahiduna) [you strive] are apparently in the narrative style, but in meaning, they are commands. The benefit of this style here is that they are being explained this matter with utmost affection, as if: 'We are telling you a highly profitable business—that you hand over your wealth and your life to Allah and receive an eternal kingdom in exchange for it.' (That is best for you, if you should know) meaning apparently this bargain will seem to you a bargain of loss because you will have to sacrifice your cash for the sake of credit, but if your vision reaches the ultimate reality, you will see that in exchange for a few of your worthless pebbles and a few borrowed moments of a transient life, you have achieved the wealth of both worlds for which there is never any decline. (That is the great success) Summarily pointing toward this wealth of both worlds, He said that if you think, you will know that this is the greatest success achieved by you. You could not achieve any greater success than this from your humble asset. The mention of this profitable business has come at another place like this: (Indeed, Allah has purchased from the believers their lives and their properties for that they will have Paradise).

(And another which you love - victory from Allah and an imminent conquest; and give good tidings to the believers) (13).
(Glad Tidings of Success and Dominance in this World). This also pointed toward a success which is going to manifest for them very soon in this world. It is said that another triumph will also be achieved by you which is going to appear soon.

(Glad Tiding of the Conquest of Makkah). This pointer, in our view, is toward the Conquest of Makkah. After the Hijrah (migration), the greatest desire embedded in the heart of every Muslim was indeed the Conquest of Makkah. Upon this depended the defeat of the actual enemies of Islam, and this was also the greatest witness to the dominance of truth. For this reason, every Muslim was desirous of it as the ultimate goal of all his struggles. The attribute of victory here has come as 'Qareeb' (imminent/near), from which it emerges that this Surah was revealed in that era when not much delay was left in the manifestation of the Conquest of Makkah. Some people have stated the time of revelation of the Surah around the Expedition of Uhud, but this cannot be correct in any way. To estimate the time of revelation of the Surah, the most suitable extraction can be this very verse, and from it, it is clearly known that it was revealed shortly before the Conquest of Makkah.

(And give good tidings to the believers) When the mention of the Conquest of Makkah came upon the tongue, opportunely Allah Almighty instructed the Prophet (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him): 'Now convey the glad tidings to the Muslims that this blessed hour is not far away. Its manifestation is going to happen soon. People should keep their morale high and wait for it.' The glad tiding of the Conquest of Makkah has appeared in multiple places in the Holy Quran through various styles. Although it is difficult to say with absolute certainty, this glad tiding has appeared in such a way that it gives an impression that perhaps this is the very first glad tiding.

(O you who have believed, be supporters of Allah , as when Jesus, the son of Mary, said to the disciples, 'Who are my supporters for Allah ?' The disciples said, 'We are supporters of Allah.' So a faction of the Children of Israel believed and a faction disbelieved. So We supported those who believed against their enemy, and they became dominant) (14).
(The Exemplary Model of the Disciples). Above in verse 5, Muslims are restricted from imitating the conduct of the Jews, warning that if you walk in their footsteps, you will remain afflicted with that same distorted vision in which they were afflicted. Now it is stated that the exemplary model for you to copy is that of the disciples, who did not deny the Messiah (peace be upon him) like the Jews did; rather, when the Messiah (peace be upon him) invited them to rise for Allah, they stood up for help and companionship in this path with complete willingness of heart, and Allah ultimately made those very devoted ones the means for the dominance of truth.

The word 'Hawari' (disciple) seems to have come into Arabic from the People of the Book. It means an active, passionate supporter and ally. It emerges from both the Holy Quran and the Gospel that when Prophet Jesus (peace be upon him) became disappointed with the attitude of the scholars and jurists of the Jews, he invited the poor, the common folk, and the fishermen at the riverbank, saying: 'O catchers of fish! Come, I will make you catchers of men.' Ultimately, from among them, a short group of three to three hundred and fifty men stood up with complete willingness of heart to cooperate in the work of dawah (proselytization). From the style of expression in (Who are my supporters for Allah?), it emerges that the Messiah (peace be upon him) said: 'I have started walking toward Allah upon the straight path told by God; whosoever among you has the courage for my companionship in this path should rise and accompany me.' The disciples responded to this call by saying 'Nahnu Ansarullah' (We are the helpers of Allah).

(So a faction of the Children of Israel believed and a faction disbelieved) The context indicates that the indefiniteness of the word 'Ta'ifah' (faction) implies a small number in one place and a large number in the other. That is, a small group from among the Children of Israel (the group of disciples) believed, and a large group remained stubborn upon its disbelief, but the support of God was attained by the believers and they ultimately became dominant over the disbelievers. It should remain clear here that ever since the denial of the call of our master Messiah (peace be upon him), the Jews never attained political authority. In the era of the Messiah (peace be upon him), they were subjects of the Romans. Then, ever since Constantine accepted Christianity, they continuously remained subjects of the Christians, and even today, when apparently they have an independent state, they are subordinate to the Christians. In the Holy Quran, this subject is explained at another place like this:
(But when Jesus felt disbelief from them, he said, 'Who are my supporters for Allah?' The disciples said, 'We are supporters of Allah. We have believed in Allah and testify that we are Muslims. Our Lord, we have believed in what You revealed and have followed the messenger, so register us among the witnesses.') (Al-Imran: 3, 52-53). With the support of Allah Almighty, the exegesis of this Surah is completed in these lines. (So praise be to Allah for that). (Rahman Abad. 14 March 1978 CE. 3 Rabi' ath-Thani 1398 AH).
